steps.title: Identify the first graph's starting point and vertex
steps.content: The first graph shows a parabola opening downward. At $x = 0$, the height is 7 feet. The vertex (maximum height) is at approximately $(0.3, 8.3)$.

steps.title: Determine the transformation for the second launch
steps.content: The second launch uses a platform that is 2 feet shorter: $7 - 2 = 5$ feet. The launch mechanism is identical, so the parabolic shape remains unchanged. The entire graph shifts vertically downward by 2 feet—every $y$-coordinate decreases by 2.

steps.title: Apply the downward shift to find the new vertex and key points
steps.content:
Shifting each point on the first graph down by 2 feet:
  • New starting height: $7 - 2 = 5$ feet at $x = 0$
  • New vertex $y$-coordinate: $8.3 - 2 = 6.3$, so vertex is at $(0.3, 6.3)$
  • New height at $x \approx 0.6$: $7 - 2 = 5$ feet
This matches a parabola passing through $(0, 5)$, with vertex at $(0.3, 6.3)$, and passing through $(0.6, 5)$. That matches choice B—B is correct.
